summary: Task API comprehensive backend testing - All 28 tests passed. Tested full CRUD operations, RBAC rules (admin-only task creation, client visibility restrictions, client status-only updates), filters (status, visibility, assignee_user_id, request_id), reorder endpoint, and completed_at auto-management.
critical_code_review_comments: Line 119-134: Task enrichment does 3 DB queries per task (assignee, creator, request). Acceptable for MVP but consider batching for scale.
No DELETE endpoint for tasks - may need to add for admin cleanup functionality
Line 91-93: Admin can technically query other org's tasks if they pass org_id param (returns empty due to org_id filter) - minor RBAC edge case
seed_data_creation: 12 test tasks created and cleaned up via MongoDB
context_for_next_testing_agent: Task API fully tested. All endpoints work: POST /api/tasks (admin only), GET /api/tasks (with RBAC visibility), PATCH /api/tasks/{id} (admin full, client status-only), PATCH /api/tasks/reorder. No pool/routing concepts in task model. Client visibility correctly enforced (client/both only). completed_at auto-managed on status changes.

test_coverage.no_pool_routing_logic: VERIFIED - no pool/routing fields in responses
